dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9680 IH: Replace UNRWA with Real Humanitarian Assistance Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Lawler (for himself, Mr. Gottheimer, Mr. McCaul, Ms. Tenney, Mr. Bacon, Mr. Fine, Mr. Stauber, Mr. Smith of New Jersey, Mr. Fitzpatrick, Mr. LaLota, Mr. Pfluger, Mr. Wilson of South Carolina, Mr. Van Drew, Mrs. Wagner, Mrs. Fedorchak, Mr. Miller of Ohio, Mr. Smith of Nebraska, Mr. Ciscomani, Mr. Loudermilk, Mr. Langworthy, Mrs. Kim, Mr. Huizenga, Mr. Hill of Arkansas, Mr. Issa, and Mr. Goldman of Texas) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Secretary State to submit to Congress a strategy to dismantle United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East operations, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Replace UNRWA with Real Humanitarian Assistance Act.

section.header: Strategy to dismantle United National Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East operations

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary of State shall submit to Congress a comprehensive strategy, coordinated with international partners and allies, to dismantle United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (in this section UNRWA) operations throughout the Middle East.

paragraph.text: a detailed plan and timeline for the phased wind-down of UNRWA operations;
an assessment of all UNRWA programs, including education, healthcare, social services, and infrastructure;
identification of appropriate entities to assume responsibility for such programs, including—
identification of entities to assume responsibility for funding such programs;
a plan to ensure continuity of services during the transition period;
mechanisms to ensure neutrality, accountability, transparency, and oversight in successor programs;
a risk-reward assessment addressing humanitarian, political, and security implications;
a diplomatic engagement plan with host countries and international partners; and
estimated costs and savings associated with the transition and recommendations for funding structures.

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after submission of the strategy, the Secretary of State shall begin implementation of such strategy.
The strategy shall be implemented in coordination with—

paragraph.text: any other implementing partners the Secretary of State determines appropriate.

subsection.text: In carrying out the implementation of the strategy, the Secretary of State shall ensure that—

paragraph.text: no interruption of critical humanitarian services occurs; and
all successor entities meet minimum standards for transparency and accountability.
